level_signals: 4+ years of experience in program management, GTM strategy and operations, business operations, management consulting, or a related discipline
Accountability for large-scale GTM programs end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of experience in program management, GTM strategy and operations, business operations, management consulting, or a related discipline
A strong track record of delivering complex, cross-functional programs in a changing, ambiguous environment end-to-end (from problem definition through rollout and adoption)

Deep problem-solving and analysis experience (solving business problems—commercial, operational, financial) in a Sales-oriented environment. You must have the ability to diagnose, simplify, and structure problems and solutions while having command of "the details".
Excellent analytical, presentation, and communication experience, influencing across all levels from front lines to C-Suite. You set a high-bar in terms of communication effectiveness.

The ability to seamlessly transition from strategy, high-level to very detailed, and a willingness to "roll up your sleeves." You work well under pressure, with a high degree of adaptability and flexibility in a fast-paced, rapidly changing environment.
Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders to align and execute on common objectives

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience operating with incomplete data and reprioritizing as business needs change

Prior experience supporting enterprise GTM motions (Sales-led and partner-led) and partnering with senior stakeholders through executive-facing cadences (QBRs, exec briefings, and account and territory planning)

Background in GTM programs that improve field execution—such as sales plays, partner co-sell motions, enablement rollouts, pipeline discipline, or launch and readiness programs across Sales, Marketing, and Product
Experience partnering with Data Science or building dashboards and automation for program observability
Familiarity with pipeline and forecasting operating rhythms (e.g., weekly forecast, MBR and QBR inputs) and improving pipeline hygiene at scale
Experience partnering closely with Sales Strategy and Operations on process and tooling improvements (e.g., dashboards, workflow automation, data quality initiatives)
Comfort with SQL or analytics tools to interrogate program and user data

scope.surface: Sales Strategy and Operations — GTM programs for Americas Sales org, Global Product Sales, and Alliances/Channels (platform partners, SIs, consultancies)
core_responsibilities: Build and drive large-scale GTM programs end-to-end—aligning stakeholders, defining the plan and operating model, and ensuring measurable outcomes
Drive the 'full-stack' of change management (stakeholder engagement, champions, communications, and enablement) so programs land successfully with Sales and Partners
Support operating rhythms that improve pipeline quality, stage discipline, and forecast consistency
Provide central project management support on high-priority global initiatives ('Big Rocks' workstreams)
Support partner-inclusive motions that embed partners into the selling process from opportunity identification through close and expansion
Build concise exec-ready readouts connecting program activity to outcomes and drive decisions in WBR, MBR, and QBR forums
Develop a roadmap for foundational requirements to operate the program efficiently and at scale
Set goals and targets for programs, run key forums and updates, and keep executives and cross-functional partners informed on progress, risks, and decisions

green_flags: Clear scope and responsibilities laid out with specificity
Explicit minimum vs. preferred qualifications distinction reduces ambiguity
High-visibility role with C-Suite exposure and exec reporting
Cross-functional breadth (Sales, Marketing, Product, Finance, RevOps, Data Science) offers broad career development
Stripe's scale and mission ('increase the GDP of the internet') are compelling draws
Candidates explicitly encouraged to apply if they meet minimum requirements
red_flags: No location, work authorization, or visa sponsorship information provided in the JD
Role demands simultaneously high-level strategic thinking and granular operational detail — may be a wide scope for one person
Payments/fintech domain experience listed as 'nice to have' but Stripe is deeply specialized — may carry more weight in practice
